steps.title: Set up the equation for the three parts
steps.content:
The line segment is divided into three parts totaling 117 cm. One part is 47 cm long, and the other two parts have equal length. Let $x$ cm be the length of each of the two equal parts. The sum of all three parts must equal 117:
$$47 + x + x = 117$$

steps.title: Solve for the length of each equal part
steps.content:
Combine like terms:
$$47 + 2x = 117$$
Subtract 47 from both sides:
$$2x = 70$$
Divide both sides by 2:
$$x = 35$$
Each of the two equal parts is 35 cm long.
